summary: SprintNote backend fully tested via pytest against public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. All 16 tests passed covering health, signup→OTP→verify→login→me auth flow, login (verified & unverified), Emergent session invalid-session 401, /api/notes CRUD (create/list/get/update/delete with persistence verification & _id exclusion), /api/folders counts, and /api/ai/rewrite for both 'Meeting Minutes' (Medium) and 'Bullet Summary' (High) styles using GPT-5.2 via Emergent LLM key. AI rewrite returns proper TITLE: prefix parsing and style-specific output (sections for meeting minutes, '•' bullets for bullet summary).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — consider migrating to lifespan handler (non-blocking, but will warn).
JWT_SECRET in .env is a placeholder ('change-me-in-prod-2026') — must be rotated to a cryptographically strong secret before production.

/api/auth/signup and /api/auth/login return dev_otp in the response body. This is acceptable for MVP/dev mode per problem statement, but the OTP must be removed from the response in production once real email delivery is wired up.
/api/ai/transcribe falls back to a hardcoded placeholder transcript if Whisper fails — this silently masks real API errors. Consider returning 502 instead so the UI can show a real error state.
current_user() helper rejects requests with no Authorization header with 401 — correct. Good _id and password/otp exclusion in projection.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates a unique test user 'test_qa_<uuid8>@sprintnote.com' (password qa123456). A note is created in folder 'Work' and deleted at the end of the run, so no residual notes remain. The test user record itself is left in MongoDB (not deleted) but is namespaced per run and harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_sprintnote_backend.py is reusable. It auto-generates a unique TEST_ email per run so it is idempotent. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (loaded via python-dotenv). For Whisper /api/ai/transcribe, no test was written because audio file is required — endpoint accepts multipart but is out of scope per main agent. Emergent OAuth happy-path requires a real Emergent session_id and was not tested; only the invalid-session 401 path was verified.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Two initial test failures during the first run were caused by an over-strict assertion in the test file (expected uppercased 'TEST_' email but backend correctly lowercases all emails on insert). Fixed in test file; backend behavior is correct.
